notes: First real local loopback row after decode/playout instrumentation; e2e estimate is sender estimated path plus receiver estimated path, not synchronized glass-to-glass.
Native preview no longer blocks on vsync and preview playout advances toward the live edge when its queue grows; estimated e2e dropped mostly from receiver playout.
Default A/V Watch latency is now 60 ms preview and 80 ms audio; normal --watch loopback uses the lower-latency path without explicit flags.
Default A/V Watch latency is now 40 ms preview and 60 ms audio; normal --watch loopback stayed healthy with no receiver late or overflow drops.
Normal stream capture no longer reads BGRA back to CPU when NV12 is already enough for software stream encoding; BGRA readback remains for recording, BMP dumps, and capture-only diagnostics.

scenario: Local loopback CLI watch + share with --stream-encoder auto
notes: Explicit auto encoder now falls back from hardware to software after persistent hardware input drops; the generated report exposes auto drop reports and fallback count.

notes: Fanout send addresses are cached and rebuilt only when targets change; two local watchers completed healthy with viewer target queue average 0 ms and max 0 ms.
